1.title: Shiromibashi Riverside — Cherry Blossoms in Spring Haze
1.subtitle: Cherry Blossoms and Red Bridge, Wasen on the Moat
1.summary: Spring is when Himeji Castle is at its most spectacular. One of the finest viewpoints, framed by cherry blossoms in full bloom, can be found right next to the Himeji City Zoo.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Along the inner moat near the zoo (near Shiromibashi Bridge)

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: The perfect moment is when the regularly scheduled Wasen passes beneath the red bridge.

tips.label: Etiquette
tips.text: This is a very popular spot with limited space. Please be considerate and share the viewing area.

1.story:
Imagine a natural frame made of pale pink Somei-Yoshino cherry blossoms in full bloom. Beyond it, the striking crimson of the Shiromibashi bridge contrasts beautifully with the water, as an elegant Wasen (traditional Japanese boat) glides peacefully along the inner moat. In the background, the majestic white Main Keep (Daitenshu) towers calmly against the spring sky. The composition of these elements overlapping in perfect harmony is truly breathtaking.

Tucked away slightly, this hidden viewpoint buzzes with a quiet, passionate energy during the few days the cherry blossoms are at their peak. If you head near the zoo, you will easily spot the exact location by the crowd of photographers with their cameras ready.
1.story_jp:
満開のソメイヨシノが形作る淡い桜色の額縁。その向こうには、水面に鮮やかなコントラストを描く真紅の「城見橋（しろみばし）」と、静かな内堀を滑るように進む風雅な和船。そして、すべての奥で春の空に泰然とそびえ立つ白亜の大天守。これらがひとつの視界に完璧なバランスで重なる構図は、まさに息を呑むほどの美しさです。

ここは少し奥まった場所にある絶景ポイントですが、桜が満開を迎える数日間は、この奇跡の瞬景を求める人々の静かな熱気に包まれます。動物園のそばまで足を運べば、たくさんの人がカメラを構えているため、すぐにその場所を見つけることができるでしょう。

2.title: Southwest Panorama — Castle in Cherry Blossom Clouds
2.subtitle: Castle in Cherry Blossom Clouds / From a Private Space
2.summary: A breathtaking panoramic view of Himeji Castle from the southwest. In the gentle spring haze, the white keep appears to float on a sea of cherry blossom clouds.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Undisclosed (photographed from a private condominium)

tips.label: Highlight
tips.text: With Koko-en Garden in the foreground and the full panorama of Himeji Castle beyond, this is a composition tourists can never see.

tips.label: Notice
tips.text: This photograph was taken from private property. The exact location is withheld. Please enjoy this exclusive spring view through this image.

2.story:
This photograph captures a spectacular, panoramic view of Himeji Castle from the southwest. As spring arrives, the city is gently enveloped in a soft "spring haze" (Harugasumi). The sight of the white castle seemingly floating on a sea of cherry blossom clouds creates a deeply relaxing and breathtakingly beautiful scene.

In the foreground, you can see the lush greenery of Koko-en, the beautiful Japanese garden built on the castle's former western residence grounds. Beyond it, the entire complex of the Main Keep stands in perfect balance.

This particular photograph was actually taken from a private condominium. Unlike the dynamic views from busy tourist spots, the uniquely relaxed and gentle atmosphere of this image comes from the fact that it represents the everyday perspective of the locals who live alongside the castle.

Out of respect for privacy, the exact location cannot be disclosed. However, through this project, we are thrilled to share with you this exclusive, privileged glimpse from a "secret premium seat" known only to those who call this city home.
2.story_jp:
姫路城を南西方向から見渡す、非常に美しい全景です。手前には姫路城西御屋敷跡庭園である「好古園」の緑が広がり、その奥に連立式天守の全貌が素晴らしいバランスでそびえ立っています。

春の訪れとともに、街全体がほんのりと「春霞（はるがすみ）」に包まれます。張り詰めた冬の空気が緩み、すべてが淡い色彩に染まるような時間。桜の雲海にふわりと浮かび上がるような白鷺城の姿は、いつまで眺めていても飽きることがありません。

実はこの一枚は、あるプライベートなマンションの一室から撮影されたものです。観光名所からのダイナミックな眺めとは異なり、この写真からどこか「ゆったりとした空気感」が伝わってくるのは、これが姫路城と共に暮らす人々の、穏やかな日常の視点だからかもしれません。プライバシーの観点から場所を明かすことはできませんが、このプロジェクトを通して、姫路の街に暮らす人だけが知る「秘密の特等席」からの景色を皆様と分かち合えればと思います。

3.title: Sannomaru — Night Cherry Blossom Frame
3.subtitle: Night Cherry Blossom White Heron / At Sannomaru Square
3.summary: On a spring evening, the illuminated Himeji Castle and night cherry blossoms create a magical collaboration. A leisurely stroll through Sannomaru Square at night is a special experience unique to this season.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Sannomaru Square

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: Using drooping cherry blossom branches as a natural picture frame with the Main Keep centered within creates a beautifully composed shot.

tips.label: Etiquette
tips.text: This area is bustling with Hanami visitors. Please be mindful of others' privacy and avoid blocking pathways.

3.story:
The collaboration of the illuminated Himeji Castle and Yozakura (cherry blossoms at night) in the spring evening is truly magical. Taking a leisurely stroll through Sannomaru Square at night is a delightful and uplifting experience unique to this season.

This particular photograph was composed by using the surrounding full-bloom cherry blossom branches as a natural picture frame, capturing the dignified Main Keep perfectly in the center.

Beyond the beauty of the blossoms and the grandeur of the architecture, the lively yet peaceful presence of people enjoying Hanami (cherry blossom viewing parties) under the trees is an essential part of the castle town's spring scenery. One day, I secretly plan to capture a sweeping photograph that includes the joyful gatherings of these people, adding a new chapter to these "Thirty-Six Views."
3.story_jp:
夜空に白く浮かび上がる大天守と、ほのかに照らされた夜桜。春の夜、三の丸広場を散策するのは、本当に気持ちが良く、心が浮き立つような楽しさがあります。

この一枚は、周囲に咲き誇る満開の桜をフレーム（額縁）のように使い、その中心に凛とした姫路城を捉える構図で撮影しました。

桜の美しさや建築の雄大さはもちろんですが、桜の下でシートを広げ、和やかにお花見を楽しむ人々の気配や賑わいもまた、城下町の春を彩る大切な風景の一部です。いつか、そんな人々の楽しげな営みも一緒に広大な一枚の写真に収め、この「三十六景」の新たな1ページとして加えたいと密かに計画しています。

4.title: Sannomaru Square — Phantom Castle in Cherry Haze
4.subtitle: Castle in Cherry Haze / At Sannomaru Square
4.summary: Getting up close to the cherry blossoms in full bloom, the great keep is intentionally rendered as beautiful bokeh, melting into the spring haze. It may not be immediately recognizable as Himeji Castle, but I know for certain that it is.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Himeji Castle Sannomaru Square

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: Get close to the cherry blossoms and shoot wide open (low f-stop) to transform even massive architecture into a dreamy bokeh.

tips.label: How to Enjoy
tips.text: Capturing a landmark in sharp focus is not the only way. Expressing the very feeling of spring air is also one of the great pleasures of castle photography.

4.story:
On a day in April when the cherry blossoms were in full bloom, I got up close to the flowers in Sannomaru Square to take this shot. The structure visible in the background is the Main Keep of Himeji Castle, though it is significantly blurred.

It might not look like a photo taken at Himeji Castle at all to others. But I know for a fact that it is.

Instead of capturing the landmark sharply for everyone to easily recognize, I intentionally reversed the roles, using the colossal castle as a luxurious background of soft color. It feels as though there is a secret, intimate dialogue that exists solely between the 400-year-old fortress and myself standing there in that exact moment. It is not just a visual record, but a capture of the spring air and my own personal memory—making it a very special piece to me.
4.story_jp:
4月、三の丸広場が一年で最も華やぐ満開の季節。ある日、私は桜の花にぐっと近づいてこの写真を撮影しました。背後に見えるのは、かなりボケていますが、紛れもなく姫路城の天守閣です。

まるで姫路城で撮ったとは思えないような写真かもしれません。しかし、私にはこれが姫路城だと分かっています。

観光名所を誰もが分かるように鮮明に切り取るのではなく、あえて主従を逆転させ、巨大な城を背景の「彩り」として贅沢に使う。そこには、400年の歴史を持つお城と、その瞬間にカメラを構えた私との間にだけ存在する、密やかな対話があるように感じています。ただの記録写真ではなく、あの日の春の空気感と私の心象風景をそのまま写し取った、とても思い入れのある一枚です。

5.title: Senhime Slope — Spring Light of Sannomaru
5.subtitle: Spring Light of Sannomaru / On the Slope to Senhime Peony Garden
5.summary: A hidden spring gem, just slightly off the main sightseeing route. From partway up the gentle slope leading to Senhime Peony Garden, looking up at the castle reveals a dreamlike vista of the white keep floating above a sea of cherry blossoms.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: On the slope to Senhime Peony Garden (coordinates: 34.836720, 134.692414)

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: Stop partway up the slope and aim your camera back toward the castle. The cherry branches will wrap around the castle from below.

tips.label: Walking Tip
tips.text: Instead of heading straight to the ticket booth from Sakuramon Gate, look for the Senhime Peony Garden sign on the western side of Sannomaru Square.

5.story:
When visiting Himeji Castle, the standard route is to enter through the Sakuramon Gate, walk past Sannomaru Square, and head straight for the main ticket booth. However, we highly recommend taking a slight detour. Try walking up the gentle slope leading to the "Senhime Peony Gardens" (Senhime Botan-en) on the western side of the square.

If you stop halfway up this slope and look back toward the Main Keep, a miraculous composition reveals itself. With layers of full-bloom cherry blossoms stretching from the foreground, the majestic white castle appears to float gracefully on a sea of pink petals.

It is a beautiful, spring-filled "detour" view, hidden right next to the main path that most visitors hurriedly walk past. This spot teaches us the profound depth of Himeji Castle and the joy of new discoveries that come from simply wandering a little off the beaten path.
5.story_jp:
姫路城を訪れた際、桜門橋を渡って三の丸広場の横を通り、そのまま天守閣の入城口へと真っ直ぐに向かうのが最も一般的な見学ルートです。しかし、そこから少しだけ寄り道をして、広場の西側に位置する「千姫ぼたん園」へと続く、なだらかな坂道を登ってみてください。

その坂の途中でふと足を止め、お城の方角を覗き込むように見上げると、この奇跡的な構図が現れます。手前から奥へ幾重にも重なる満開の桜の下から、白亜の大天守がふわりと顔を出し、まるで淡いピンク色の海に城が浮かんでいるかのような一枚を撮ることができるのです。

多くの人が足早に通り過ぎてしまうメインルートのすぐそばに、これほど春爛漫の美しい「寄り道」の景色が隠されている。姫路城の懐の深さと、歩き回ることで見つかる新しい発見の喜びを教えてくれる素晴らしいスポットです。

location.name: On the slope to Senhime Peony Garden

6.title: Hakuro School — View of Youth
6.subtitle: View from Hakuro School / From the Perimeter Road near Hakuro Elementary and Junior High School
6.summary: Behind the students exercising on the athletic field, a World Heritage site stands as if it were the most natural thing in the world. This place captures the luxurious everyday scene of the castle quietly watching over the youth of Himeji.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Perimeter road around Hakuro Elementary and Junior High School

tips.label: Scene Appeal
tips.text: The contrast between vibrant modern school life and a 400-year-old historic structure.

tips.label: Absolute Rule
tips.text: This is an active educational facility. Unauthorized entry and any photography directed at students is strictly prohibited. Enjoy the scenery only from the public sidewalk.

6.story:
Decades ago, when I was a junior high school student, I visited this very ground several times to compete in city tennis tournaments. I still fondly remember playing matches on the courts that used to be here.

Although the school is now a combined elementary and junior high school (Hakuro Elementary and Junior High School), the magnificent view of Himeji Castle towering over the athletic field remains exactly the same. The students who attend this school might take this incredible "luck" for granted, not fully realizing what a privilege it is to spend their daily lives watched over by a World Heritage site.

However, years from now, when they are adults looking back at the smartphone photos from their school days, they will notice that the majestic white castle was always standing beautifully behind their lively, smiling faces. Imagining the moment they make that heartwarming discovery fills me with excitement and warmth. The castle quietly watches over not just the history of the city, but also the precious memories of its people's youth.
6.story_jp:
何十年も前、私がまだ中学生だった頃。姫路市のテニスの大会で、このグラウンドにあったコートで何度か試合をした懐かしい記憶があります。

現在、この場所は統合により小中一貫校（白鷺小中学校）へと姿を変えましたが、グラウンドから雄大な姫路城を見上げる素晴らしい景色は当時のままです。この学び舎に通う生徒たちは、毎日お城に見守られながら過ごすという「幸運」を、今は当たり前のこととして特に意識していないかもしれません。

しかし何年か後、大人になってからふと学生時代のスマホの写真を見返したとき、生き生きと笑う自分たちの背後に、いつもあの白亜の城が美しく佇んでいたことに気がつくはずです。その事実に巡り会う彼らの未来の瞬間を想像すると、なんだかとってもワクワクして、温かい気持ちになります。お城は、街の歴史だけでなく、人々の青春の記憶もこうして静かに見守り続けているのです。

location.name: Near Hakuro Elementary and Junior High School (from perimeter road)

7.title: Otokoyama Heights — Tower Complex in Fresh Green
7.subtitle: Fresh Green of Otokoyama / At Otokoyama Reservoir Park
7.summary: Himeji Castle shows an entirely different face depending on the angle. From the summit of Otokoyama, reached after climbing 198 steps, you can see all three small keeps and find yourself perfectly eye-level with the Main Keep.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Otokoyama Reservoir Park (summit plaza)

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: From this angle, you can capture all three small keeps: West Small Keep to the lower right, Inui Small Keep in the center, and East Small Keep in the far left.

tips.label: Tips
tips.text: Reaching this view requires climbing 198 steep steps. Wear comfortable shoes and bring water.

7.story:
The architectural greatness of Himeji Castle lies in the fact that the Main Keep does not stand alone. It is connected to three smaller keeps by roofed passages.

The view from Otokoyama is special for two main reasons. First, looking from this northwestern direction, you can see all the small keeps simultaneously: the West Small Keep on the lower right, the Inui Small Keep closest to you in the center foreground, and the East Small Keep tucked away in the deep left. This layout perfectly highlights an overwhelming architectural complexity and formidable defense seen in few other castles.

Second is the brilliant reversal of elevation. The summit of Otokoyama, reached after a breathless climb up exactly 198 steep steps, sits at an elevation of approximately 57.5 meters. This is actually over 10 meters higher than the stone base of Himeyama (approx. 45.8 meters) where the castle stands. Because of this, you don't look up at the castle. Instead, you look slightly down upon the intricate, overlapping roofs of the three smaller keeps, while finding yourself perfectly eye-level with the middle floors of the Main Keep. It is an unparalleled premium seat to accurately grasp the true three-dimensional shape and massive scale of the fortress.
7.story_jp:
姫路城の建築的な偉大さは、大天守が単独で建っているのではなく、3つの小天守が渡櫓（わたりやぐら）で結ばれた「連立式天守」である点にあります。

男山からの眺めが特別な理由は大きく2つあります。1つ目は、この北西の方角から城を望むと、大天守の右下に「西小天守」、男山に最も近い手前中央に「乾小天守」、そして左の奥に「東小天守」と、連立するすべての小天守の配置を一度に捉えることができる点です。他に類を見ない圧倒的な複雑さと、堅牢な要塞としての姿がこの見事な配置から浮かび上がります。

2つ目は、その絶妙な「高さの逆転」です。息を切らして198段の階段を登り切った男山の頂上は、標高約57.5m。これは、姫路城が建つ「姫山」の基盤（標高約45.8m）よりも10m以上高い位置にあります。そのため、城を見上げるのではなく、眼下に3つの小天守の複雑な屋根の重なりを見下ろしつつ、そびえ立つ大天守の中層部とまさに「水平に目が合う」視線となるのです。城の真の立体感とスケールを最も正確に把握できる、比類なき特等席と言えるでしょう。

8.title: Tokoji Cemetery — Twilight and White Heron
8.subtitle: The White Castle Beyond the Silence
8.summary: The beautifully and reverently maintained Tokoji Cemetery. Walking to the uppermost tier and turning around, Himeji Castle quietly reveals itself beyond the silence.

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: A super-telephoto lens equivalent to 400mm is required (this shot was taken using Xiaomi 15 Ultra telephoto mode).

tips.label: Respect & Etiquette
tips.text: This is not a tourist spot but a sacred place of prayer. Maintain silence and ensure individual names on gravestones are not captured.

8.story: During the "magic hour"—the fleeting time around sunset when the sky is painted in mystical gradients of color—the white majestic keep seems to gently float above the darkening silhouette of the city. It is a surreal and breathtaking sight. One cannot help but feel a sense of peaceful envy for those resting in this quiet high ground, knowing they can gaze upon this beautiful castle for eternity. This view truly reflects the profound, spiritual connection between the locals and their beloved castle.
8.story_jp: 日没前後、空が神秘的な色彩のグラデーションに染まる「マジックアワー」。暮れゆく街のシルエットの向こうで、白亜の天守がふわりと浮かび上がる光景は、現実離れした美しさを持っています。この静かな高台で永遠の眠りにつく人々が、いつでも遠くに美しい城を眺めていられることを、少し羨ましくも感じる——そんな、城と人々の深い精神的な繋がりを感じさせる特別な「景」です。

13.title: Otemae Rooftop — Autumn Brocade Below the Castle
13.subtitle: Autumn Colors of the Castle Grounds from the Egret Himeji Rooftop
13.summary: Adjacent to Otemae Park, Egret Himeji offers a rooftop observation deck — the closest building to the castle — providing a premium sky-high seat to survey the entire castle grounds.

tips.label: Capture the View
tips.text: A wide-angle lens or smartphone panorama function is recommended.

tips.label: Binoculars
tips.text: Free binoculars are available to observe the intricate details of the castle's architecture.

tips.label: Facility Courtesy
tips.text: This is a public relaxation space. Please be considerate of others when using tripods.

13.story:
In this expansive, unobstructed panorama, the autumn scenery is particularly breathtaking. The entire castle grounds are dyed in fiery reds and brilliant golds, surrounding the majestic white Main Keep. This stunning gradient of autumn colors (Kinshu) can only be fully captured from this elevated vantage point.

Additionally, free binoculars are available on the rooftop. Looking through the lenses, you can clearly see the intricate details of the castle's architecture and even the people walking through the vast grounds, giving you a real sense of the castle's overwhelming scale. We highly recommend trying them to experience the grandeur firsthand.
13.story_jp:
視界を遮るもののない広大なパノラマのなかで、特に息を呑むのが秋の情景です。白亜の大天守を取り囲むように、城域全体が燃えるような赤や鮮やかな黄金色に染まりゆく「錦秋（きんしゅう）」のグラデーションは、この高台からでなければその全貌を捉えることはできません。

また、ここには無料で利用できる双眼鏡が設置されています。レンズを覗き込めば、天守閣の精巧な造りや、広大な城内を歩く人々の姿まで鮮明に確認でき、お城の圧倒的な巨大さを肌で感じることができます。ぜひ、ご自身の目でその迫力を体感してください。

14.title: Kita-Segakushimon — Hidden Water Mirror
14.subtitle: Castle in the Water Mirror / At Kita-Segakushimon Ruins
14.summary: A secret scenic spot reachable only by those who have walked every corner of Himeji Castle. By a small pond near the famous spring Sagi-no-Shimizu, the white keep is miraculously reflected upside down in a water mirror.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Outside the Kita-Segakushimon Gate ruins (small pond near Sagi-no-Shimizu)

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: Choose a calm, windless day for the water surface to become a perfect mirror, capturing the inverted Himeji Castle.

tips.label: Walking Tip
tips.text: You cannot find this from the main path through Himeyama Park. During autumn foliage season, seek it out like a treasure hunt.

14.story:
This spot is like a hidden stage within the castle grounds; if you aren't actively looking for it, you will easily walk right past. When strolling around the inner moat, most people simply follow the walking path through Himeyama Park. However, you cannot see this view from that path. It is only when you intentionally step "outside" through the stone walls of the Kita-Segakushi Gate ruins that this secret landscape reveals itself.

There lies Sagi-no-Shimizu. The surface of the small pond next to it acts as a canvas, reflecting the castle like a flawless mirror. During the autumn foliage season, the surrounding trees turn vibrant shades of red and yellow, creating a breathtaking, miniature-garden-like masterpiece of the white castle and its reflection. Because it is so easily overlooked, it is a hidden gem that we highly recommend seeking out.
14.story_jp:
この場所は、相当意識して探さないと通り過ぎてしまう、姫路城における「隠しステージ」のような場所です。

城の周囲を内堀に沿ってぐるりと回る際、多くの人はそのまま姫山公園の遊歩道を通り抜けてしまいます。しかし、そこからはこの景色は見えません。かつての城の出入り口であった「北勢隠門跡（きたせがくしもんあと）」の石垣をあえて「外」へと抜け出したとき、初めてこの秘密の風景が目の前に広がります。

そこにあるのは「鷺の清水（さぎのしみず）」と呼ばれる井戸の跡。そのすぐ横にある小さな池の水面がキャンバスとなり、姫路城の壮麗な姿を鏡のように映し出すのです。周囲の木々が色づく紅葉の季節には、赤や黄色の錦秋と白亜の城、そしてその水鏡という、息を呑むような箱庭的絶景に出会うことができます。気が付きにくい場所だからこそ、絶対に訪れてほしいと強くおすすめする名スポットです。

15.title: Art Museum Garden — Autumn Colors and Red Brick
15.subtitle: Changing Reds and the Eternal White Heron / At Himeji City Museum of Art
15.summary: As autumn deepens, the museum's elegant red brick and the surrounding autumn foliage create a beautiful gradient, forming a stunning color contrast with the white Himeji Castle towering behind.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Himeji City Museum of Art, front garden plaza

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: Layer three elements: autumn foliage foreground, red brick mid-ground, Main Keep background for maximum color contrast.

tips.label: Historical Perspective
tips.text: Consider the contrast of time between the 400-year-old castle and this building that has changed roles through the ages.

15.story:
As autumn deepens, the elegant red bricks of the Himeji City Museum of Art and the surrounding foliage create a beautiful gradient of autumn colors. This vibrant display forms a striking contrast with the majestic white Himeji Castle towering in the background.

This beautiful red brick building was originally constructed in the Meiji era as a military arsenal and clothing depot for the former Japanese Army. Having survived the fires of war, it was repurposed as the "Himeji City Hall" to support the city's post-war reconstruction. Finally, it was reborn into its current role as a "Museum of Art," a sanctuary of peace and culture.

Himeji Castle, on the other hand, has stood unchanged for over 400 years. The grand Main Keep seems to have quietly and gently watched over this red brick building at its feet as it transformed from a military facility to a civic center, and finally to an art museum.

The view from here is not merely a brilliant visual contrast of red and white; it is a profound, poetic reflection of "the unchanging" versus "the changing" — a beautiful testament to the very history the city of Himeji has navigated.
15.story_jp:
姫路城の東側に広がる姫路市立美術館。秋を迎えると、庭園の木々が色づき、建物の赤レンガと溶け合うように見事な錦秋のグラデーションを描き出します。そして、その燃えるような色彩の背後には、一点の曇りもない白亜の大天守が静かにそびえ立っています。

この美しき赤レンガの建物は、明治時代に「旧陸軍の兵器庫・被服庫」として建てられました。戦火をくぐり抜けた後は、戦後復興を支える「姫路市役所」として市民のために働き、そして現在は平和と文化の象徴である「美術館」へと、時代に合わせてその役割を大きく変えてきました。

築城から400年以上、決して変わることなくそこに在り続ける姫路城。大天守は、足元で軍事施設から市役所、そして美術館へと生まれ変わっていくこの赤レンガの建物を、まるで親のようにずっと優しく見守ってきたのでしょう。この場所からの眺めは、赤と白の鮮やかなコントラストであると同時に、「不変」と「変化」という、姫路の街が歩んできた歴史そのものの美しい対比なのです。

location.name: Himeji City Museum of Art, Front Garden

16.title: Senhime Path — Autumn Along the Moat
16.subtitle: Autumn Along the Moat / Along the Inner Moat (Senhime Path)
16.summary: Where exactly is this? Very few could identify this location, even among local Himeji residents. It is a best-kept secret walking path where the quiet everyday life of the castle town blends with breathtaking autumn foliage.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Along the inner moat, Senhime Path

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: Include the path railing and stream in the foreground, layering autumn foliage and Nishi-no-Maru behind for rich depth.

tips.label: Walking Tip
tips.text: Very close to View No. 14. Map apps may show an offset location, so use the coordinates and listen for the stream.

16.story:
"Where exactly is this?" — Even among local Himeji residents, very few could correctly identify the location just by looking at this photograph. It is a best-kept secret walking path where the quiet, everyday life of the castle town blends seamlessly with breathtaking autumn foliage.

Located along the western inner moat is a tranquil promenade known as "Senhime's Path" (Senhime-no-Komichi). This spot offers a deep, atmospheric elegance (fuzei) that differs entirely from the grand, overwhelming scale of the main castle views.

As autumn deepens, the trees turn vibrant shades of yellow and fiery red. The handrail visible in the foreground belongs to Senhime's Path, running alongside a clear, murmuring stream. Combined with the traditional townhouses (machiya) of the neighboring Korikicho district and a subtle glimpse of the westernmost edge of the Nishi-no-Maru (Western Bailey) peeking through the leaves, the scene resembles a flawless traditional Japanese painting.

While dynamic views featuring the Main Keep are magnificent, it is in these understated, quiet landscapes along the moat that you can truly feel the profound depth of Himeji Castle — a fortress that has peacefully coexisted with the daily lives of the townspeople for over 400 years.
16.story_jp:
姫路城の西側、内堀に沿って続く「千姫の小径（せんひめのこみち）」と呼ばれる静かな遊歩道。ここは、姫路城の壮大なスケール感とはまた違う、しっとりとした城下町の「風情」を色濃く残す秘密の場所です。

秋が深まると、木々が鮮やかな黄色や燃えるような赤に染まり、静かな水面を彩ります。写真の手前に見える手すりが千姫の小径のもので、そこを流れる澄んだ小川、隣接する小利木町（こりきちょう）の伝統的な町家、そして木々の隙間からひっそりと顔を覗かせる西の丸の西端。これらの要素が一つに溶け合った情景は、まるで一枚の美しい日本画のようです。

大天守が主役のダイナミックな風景も素晴らしいですが、こうした何気ない堀端の風景にこそ、400年にわたって人々の暮らしと共にあった姫路城の「奥深さ」が宿っています。喧騒から離れ、小川のせせらぎを聞きながらこの紅葉を眺める時間は、格別の体験となるはずです。

20.title: Otemae Street — Ribbon of Winter Light
20.subtitle: Otemae Illumination / On Otemae Street
20.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN
20.summary: After gazing down at the path of light from the station observatory (View No. 21), you walk that very illuminated road. Looking up as the castle looms close reveals a breathtaking winter spectacle.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Near the crosswalk at Otemae Street and Egret Himeji intersection

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: Tripods cannot be used here. Increase shutter speed and capture handheld the moment the pedestrian signal turns green.

tips.label: Safety
tips.text: This is a busy intersection. Tripods and sitting down are dangerous and strictly prohibited. Prioritize safety.

20.story:
After gazing down at the path of light from the station's observation deck (View No. 21), you can walk that very illuminated path toward the castle. As you draw closer, the sheer scale of the fortress towering ahead is a breathtaking winter spectacle.

Just a short distance from the castle, at the intersection in front of Egret Himeji, you look up to find the illuminated Main Keep standing divinely at the end of the sparkling tree-lined street. The beauty of this moment is truly awe-inspiring.

As a photographer, the immediate instinct is to set up a tripod and take a careful, long-exposure shot of this perfect composition. However, because this is a busy intersection on the city's main street, doing so is both impossible and dangerous. Instead, in that brief moment when the pedestrian signal turns green, the shot was taken handheld at a fast shutter speed. It is precisely because of the constraint of not being able to use a tripod that this photograph captures a miraculous, fleeting 'view' — one infused with the vibrant energy of the street and the photographer's own tension.
20.story_jp:
第弐拾壱景で紹介した姫路駅前の「キャッスルビュー」から、お城に向かって真っ直ぐに伸びる大手前通り。冬の時期、美しく彩られたイルミネーションの並木道を歩いていくと、徐々に姫路城がその巨大な姿を現します。

お城まであと少しというイーグレ姫路前の交差点。ここでふと見上げると、光の道の先にライトアップされた大天守が神々しくそびえ立っており、あまりの美しさに思わず息を呑みます。

写真家としては、この完璧な構図の前に三脚を据え、時間をかけて丁寧にシャッターを切りたいという衝動に駆られます。しかし、ここは街のメインストリートの交差点。当然それは叶いません。そこで、歩行者信号が青に変わったその一瞬、シャッター速度を上げて手持ちで素早く撮影を行いました。三脚を使えないという制約があるからこそ生まれた、街の躍動感と撮影者の緊張感が封じ込められた、奇跡的な一瞬の「景」です。

21.title: Station Heights — Year-End Path of Light
21.subtitle: Castle View Illumination from the Station Observatory
21.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN
21.summary: Himeji Station, dramatically transformed when the railway was elevated from ground level. The wooden observation deck Castle View is the finest modern viewpoint in Himeji.

tips.label: Capture the Light
tips.text: A medium telephoto lens (50mm-85mm) compresses the street depth for a more impactful shot.

tips.label: History Meets Modern
tips.text: The winter illumination period (late November to late December) and New Year's Eve offer the most striking moments.

tips.label: Etiquette
tips.text: This is a busy observation deck. When using tripods, please be mindful of pedestrian traffic.

21.story:
Stretching straight ahead toward the castle is the main street, "Otemae-dori." Before the station was elevated, locals affectionately called it the "50-Meter Road." Today, lined with modern high-rise buildings, the street's trees are adorned with beautiful illuminations during the winter and year-end season (New Year's Eve). At the end of this dazzling path of light sits Himeji Castle, itself illuminated and floating pure white against the night sky. The sight is nothing short of spectacular.

Over 400 years since the castle's construction, this specific view represents the most dramatic evolution of the city. What would the samurai of the past think if they could see this glowing modern castle town, with their beloved White Heron still standing proudly at its center? Letting your imagination transcend time is one of the true joys of this observatory.
21.story_jp:
ここから真っ直ぐにお城へと伸びるメインストリート「大手前通り」。駅が高架化される以前は「50m道路」と呼ばれ親しまれていたこの大通りの両側には、今では近代的なビルが立ち並びます。そして冬の歳末（大晦日）の時期には、街路樹が美しいイルミネーションの光で彩られます。その光の道の終着点に、同じくライトアップされて夜空に白く浮かび上がる姫路城が鎮座する光景は、息を呑むほどの美しさです。

築城から400年。この場所からの眺めは、姫路の街で最も劇的な変化を遂げた風景と言えるでしょう。もし、かつての戦国武将たちがこの光り輝く現代の城下町と、変わらず凛とそびえる白鷺の姿を見たら一体どう思うだろうか——そんな時空を超えた想像を巡らせるのも、この展望台ならではの醍醐味です。

22.title: Nagoyama Cemetery — First Light of the New Year
22.subtitle: Castle at First Light, 2026 First Sunrise / At Nagoyama
22.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN
22.summary: The First Sunrise of the New Year. The white keep, divinely illuminated by the fresh morning sun viewed from Nagoyama, is a landscape worthy of heralding a new year.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Nagoyama Cemetery hilltop

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: Sunrise on New Year's Day is after 7:00 AM. Otokoyama gets very crowded, making Nagoyama an excellent alternative.

tips.label: Etiquette
tips.text: This is a sacred place. Maintain silence, respect the gravestones, and dress warmly.

22.story:
The "First Sunrise" (Hatsuhinode) of the New Year is a deeply cherished tradition in Japan. The sight of the majestic white keep bathed in the fresh, golden light of the year's first morning, viewed from Nagoyama, is a perfectly divine landscape to mark a new beginning.

On New Year's Day 2026, the original plan was to capture the sunrise and the castle together from the highly popular Otokoyama Reservoir. However, the unexpected density of the crowds prompted a quick pivot to Plan B: Nagoyama Cemetery.

Since the winter sunrise isn't until around 7:00 AM, it doesn't require an extreme early wake-up call—though securing a prime spot at Otokoyama clearly demands much stronger dedication (a definite goal for next year!).

Nevertheless, adjusting the destination to Nagoyama was incredibly rewarding. Watching Himeji Castle gently take on a golden hue as it caught the first rays of the sun was a breathtaking and profoundly spiritual experience. While Otokoyama might offer a closer visual alignment of the sun and the castle, the pristine New Year's air and the radiant White Heron viewed from Nagoyama make this a truly magnificent scene worth seeking out.
22.story_jp:
2026年の元旦。初日の出と姫路城を一枚に収めようと、王道の撮影スポットである「男山配水池公園」へ向かいましたが、すでに想像以上の人出。そこで急遽、次善の策として考えていたこの「名古山霊園」へと目的地を変更しました。

冬の日の出は朝7時頃と比較的遅いため、過酷な早起きは必要ありませんでしたが、男山の特等席を確保するにはさらなる覚悟が必要だったと痛感しました（来年こそはもっと早く起きるぞ！と心に誓っています）。

しかし、名古山へ場所を移した判断は決して間違っていませんでした。昇る初日の出の光を一身に受け、ほんのりと黄金色に染まりゆく姫路城の姿は息を呑むほど神々しく、静かな感動に包まれました。太陽とお城のシルエットをより近づけて撮るなら男山に軍配が上がりますが、名古山から望む清らかな新年の空気と輝く白鷺の姿もまた、間違いなく「行った甲斐がある」珠玉の情景です。

23.title: Sannomaru Square — Rainbow at the Fire Review
23.subtitle: New Year Water Salute / At Himeji Castle Sannomaru Square
23.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN
23.summary: The annual Himeji City Fire Department New Year Review held every January. The beautiful synchronized water discharge and the miraculous rainbow is a special view exclusive to this day.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Himeji Castle Sannomaru Square

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: The ultimate shutter chance is when sunlight breaks through the clouds and hits the water arcs, creating a rainbow.

tips.label: Event Guide
tips.text: This spectacle is only visible on the event day in early January each year (January 11 in 2026).

23.story:
January 11, 2026, marked the highly anticipated annual Dezomeshiki (New Year's Fire Review) by the Himeji City Fire Brigade. Held at Sannomaru Square, this traditional event gathers city officials and countless citizens to watch the impressive results of the firefighters' training, as well as charming performances by local children.

The weather that day was incredibly fickle and dramatic, alternating unpredictably between sudden snow flurries and clear blue skies. Despite the changing skies, the climax of the event — the synchronized mass water discharge — was a moment of pure beauty. As the water powerfully arced into the air, the sunlight broke through the clouds, catching the spray and creating a miraculous, breathtaking rainbow.

Watching these highly trained firefighters in action under the blue sky was deeply reassuring. Knowing that they stand ready to respond to any emergency fire gives us the profound confidence that this irreplaceable wooden castle, and the city itself, are in the safest of hands.
23.story_jp:
2026年1月11日。私が心待ちにしていた、姫路市消防団による新春恒例の「出初式」の日です。

この日の天候は非常にドラマチックでした。雪が舞ったかと思えば、スッと青空が覗くという、冬特有の目まぐるしく変わる不思議な空模様。そんな中、多くの市民や来賓が見守る三の丸広場では、訓練を重ねた消防団員たちの凛々しい姿や、子どもたちの可愛らしい演技が披露され、会場は温かい活気に包まれました。

そして迎えたクライマックスの一斉放水。空高く幾重にも放たれた美しい水柱に、雲間から射した陽光が反射し、見事な虹が架かった瞬間は思わず息を呑むほど感動的でした。

青空の下で躍動する消防員たちの頼もしい姿。彼らの日々の鍛錬があればこそ、万が一の出火時にも、このかけがえのない木造の白鷺城と私たちの街は確実に守り抜かれるだろう――そんな強い安心感と誇りを胸に抱かせてくれる、新春の素晴らしい伝統行事です。

24.title: Sannomaru Square — Guardians Under Blue Sky
24.subtitle: Rescue Team Performance / At Himeji Castle Sannomaru Square
24.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN
24.summary: Elite rescue workers perform their breathtaking aerial display, soaring to heights rivaling the great keep itself. A modern view both awe-inspiring and beautiful, set against the blue sky and the famous castle.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Himeji Castle Sannomaru Square

tips.label: Highlight
tips.text: Highly trained rescue workers performing powerful and beautiful aerial maneuvers at heights rivaling the Main Keep.

tips.label: Event Warmth
tips.text: While the training demonstration is performed with intense focus, humorous live commentary introducing each worker brings warm laughter to the audience.

24.story:
This scene, like View No. 23, was captured during the Himeji City Fire Brigade's New Year's Fire Review (Dezomeshiki) on January 11, 2026.

The weather that day was incredibly harsh, with strong gusts of wind and occasional snow flurries. Due to safety concerns in the rough conditions, the fire trucks had to operate their aerial ladders at a lower height than originally planned. Yet, even under such challenging circumstances, the highly trained rescue workers delivered an astonishing mid-air performance. Watching them dynamically "dance" in the clear blue sky, seemingly reaching the very height of the grand Main Keep, was a breathtakingly beautiful and powerful sight.

What made this performance even more captivating was the excellent live announcement echoing through the venue. The announcer introduced each airborne rescue worker not just by name, but by sharing charming, humorous details about their personalities and hobbies. This warm commentary brought delightful smiles and laughter from the crowd.

It was a perfect contrast: the display of rigorous, life-saving training balanced by lighthearted humor. Surrounded by the warm, unified atmosphere of the venue, I felt a deep sense of pride and profound reassurance, knowing that the beautiful city of Himeji and its historic castle are protected by such wonderful, dedicated people.
24.story_jp:
第弐拾参景（No. 23）と同じく、2026年1月11日の「姫路市消防出初式」での一コマです。

この日は時折雪が舞い、強い風が吹き荒れる非常に厳しい天候でした。安全を考慮し、はしご車の高さを予定よりも低く制限せざるを得ない状況だったそうですが、そんな悪条件の中でも、高度に訓練されたレスキュー隊員たちは見事な空中演技を繰り広げました。澄み切った青空の下、まるで大天守と同じ高さで宙を舞うかのような彼らの躍動的な姿は、ハッとするほど美しく、力強いものでした。

そして、この日の演舞をさらに魅力的なものにしていたのが、会場内に響く素晴らしいアナウンスです。宙を舞う隊員一人ひとりの人となりや趣味などを、ユーモアを交えながら細やかに紹介していくその温かい実況に、見守る観客からは微笑ましい笑いが漏れていました。

厳しい訓練の成果と、緊張をほぐすユーモラスな言葉の絶妙なコントラスト。会場全体が一体となって盛り上がる温かい空気の中で、「ああ、姫路の街とこの美しいお城は、こういう素晴らしい人たちに守られているのだな」と、胸が熱くなるような安心感と誇りを感じた一日でした。

25.title: Floating Castle in the Dark Night: The White Majesty
25.subtitle: White Majesty in the Dark / In front of Sangoku-bori
25.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN
25.summary: Himeji Castle floats in the pitch-black night, bathed in pure white light. Captured during an exclusive, limited-access night tour, this image reveals a mystical and solemn "White Heron" completely different from its daytime appearance.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: In front of Sangoku-bori moat, Himeji Castle

tips.label: Photo Tips
tips.text: Since you must shoot handheld in the dark, maximize your camera's night mode or image stabilization, hold your breath, and keep as steady as possible.

tips.label: Strict Rule
tips.text: This view is from a special night tour. The bringing and use of tripods or monopods is strictly prohibited here. Please respect the rules while enjoying the night walk.

25.story:
Bathed in white light, Himeji Castle emerges from the darkness of the night. Its appearance exudes a mystical, ethereal beauty that is entirely different from its imposing daytime presence. From right to left, the entire complex of the Main Keep, West Small Keep, and Inui Small Keep stands out sharply against the pitch-black sky.

I took this photograph while participating in a special Himeji Castle Night Tour. With only about 10 participants and no other tourists around, it felt as though we had rented out the entire castle for ourselves—an incredibly luxurious and tranquil experience.

I shot this from just in front of a small moat called "Sangoku-bori." At this hour, the castle was illuminated solely by white light. In the next photograph (View No. 26), you will see it lit up in colorful lights. However, true to its name, the "White Heron Castle" (Shirasagi-jo), I personally believe this pure, unadulterated white light suits it best.
25.story_jp:
夜の闇の中に、白い光を纏って浮かび上がる姫路城。その姿は、昼間の堂々とした威厳とはまったく異なる、神秘的で幻想的な美しさを湛えています。右から左へ、大天守、西小天守、乾小天守と連なる連立天守の全景が、ライトアップによって漆黒の夜空にくっきりと浮かび上がります。

この写真は、特別な姫路城のナイトツアーに参加した際に撮影したものです。参加者はわずか10名ほど。一般の観光客は誰一人おらず、まるで夜の姫路城を貸切にしたかのような、極めて贅沢で静謐な時間を過ごしました。

撮影場所は「三国堀」という小さな堀の手前です。この時間帯、お城はまだ純白のライトで照らされているだけでした。実は、次にご紹介する写真（第弐拾録景）ではカラフルにライトアップされた様子をご覧いただけるのですが、やはり「白鷺城」と呼ばれるだけあって、私個人としてはこの混じり気のない「白」の光こそが、一番お城に似合っていると感じています。

location.name: In front of Sangoku-bori moat, Himeji Castle

26.title: Bewitching Polychrome at Sangoku-bori
26.subtitle: Bewitching Transformation / In front of Sangoku-bori
26.summary: At 8:00 PM, Himeji Castle's illumination transforms from pure white to a bewitching display of purple and blue. Witnessing this dramatic daily change from the front row at Sangoku-bori is a luxury exclusive to the private night tour.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: In front of Sangoku-bori moat, Himeji Castle (Same spot as No. 25).

tips.label: Photo Tips
tips.text: Colorful LED lights can easily overexpose. Slightly lower the exposure (underexpose) to preserve the rich, bewitching color gradations.

tips.label: Strict Rule
tips.text: To reiterate from View No. 25, this was taken inside the closed grounds during a special night tour. The bringing and use of tripods or monopods are strictly prohibited here.

26.story:
This shot was taken from the exact same location as View No. 25. The castle's illumination is programmed to change regularly. At the stroke of 8:00 PM, the pure white light dramatically shifts to a colorful, almost "bewitching" blend of blues and reddish-purples.

As I mentioned previously, I personally believe that the pure white light is the most fitting for the "White Heron Castle." Yet, considering current trends, I couldn't help but feel that many overseas tourists might actually prefer these slightly flashier, more entertaining colors.

While this lighting change itself can be seen from outside the castle grounds, being able to photograph this bewitching figure from up close at "Sangoku-bori" after the gates have closed is a privilege reserved only for participants of the exclusive night tour. With the strict no-tripod rule, the tension of shooting handheld only added to the profound experience of exploring the castle at night.
26.story_jp:
第弐拾伍景（No. 25）と全く同じ場所からの撮影です。姫路城のライトアップは定期的に仕様が変わるようになっており、夜8時になると純白の光から、青や赤紫のような少し「妖艶」とも言えるカラフルな色合いへと劇的に変化します。

前にも書いた通り、私個人としてはやはり「白鷺城」と呼ばれる姫路城には、純白のライトが一番似合うと思っています。しかし、昨今の状況を鑑みると、海外から訪れる多くの観光客の方々には、こうした少し派手でエンターテインメント性のある色彩の方が好まれるのかもしれないな、とも感じました。

このライトアップの変化自体は城外からも見ることができますが、閉門後の「三国堀の手前」という至近距離からこの妖艶な姿を撮影できるのは、限られた人数のナイトツアーに参加したからこその特権です。三脚禁止という制約の中、手持ち撮影の緊張感とともに、普段は立ち入れない夜のお城の奥深さを味わうことができました。

location.name: In front of Sangoku-bori moat, Himeji Castle

26.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN

27.title: The Silent Shogun Slope at Night
27.subtitle: Night at Shogun Slope / The stone steps leading to Ha-no-Mon Gate
27.summary: Commonly known as "Shogun Slope," a famous filming location for historical samurai dramas. Normally packed with tourists, this miraculous shot captures the iconic spot completely deserted and beautifully illuminated during an exclusive annual tour.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Commonly known as "Shogun Slope" (Stone steps leading to Ha-no-Mon Gate)

tips.label: Photo Tips
tips.text: The classic composition is to shoot looking up the slope, framing the earthen wall with its loopholes (hazama) on the right and the Main Keep in the center background.

tips.label: Exclusive Background
tips.text: This photo was actually taken during the "Himeji Castle Premium Night Tour," an exclusive event held for only three days once a year. A completely empty Shogun Slope at night is a phantom sight you can only witness during this limited opportunity.

27.story:
These stone steps lead up to the "Ha-no-Mon Gate," flanked on the right by an earthen wall lined with hazama (loopholes for firing matchlocks and arrows), with a direct view of the Main Keep straight ahead. Because this location frequently appeared as a scene inside Edo Castle in the classic historical TV drama Abarenbo Shogun (The Unfettered Shogun), it has come to be affectionately known as "Shogun Slope."

It is one of the most popular photo spots in the castle, constantly bustling with people during the day. However, by participating in the "Himeji Castle Premium Night Tour"—a highly exclusive event held over just three days once a year—I was miraculously able to monopolize and photograph this illuminated, completely deserted slope.

The silent Shogun Slope at night possesses an intense atmosphere, making you feel as if you've truly slipped back in time to the Edo period. By the way, the branches extending from the stone wall on the left belong to a cherry tree. In just a little while, as the season progresses, one can only imagine the breathtaking view when night cherry blossoms add their colors to this empty slope.
27.story_jp:
右手に狭間（さま：鉄砲や弓を撃つための穴）が並ぶ土塀を据え、正面に姫路城の天守を望みながら「はの門」へと上っていく石段。ここは、松平健さん主演の時代劇『暴れん坊将軍』で、江戸城内の風景として度々登場したことから、いつしか「将軍坂」という通称で呼ばれるようになりました。

お城の中でも屈指の人気撮影スポットであり、日中は途切れることなく多くの人で賑わっています。しかしこの一枚は、年に一度、わずか3日間にわたって開催される「姫路城プレミアムナイトツアー」に参加したことで、奇跡的に誰もいないライトアップされた状態を独占して撮影することができたものです。

静まり返った夜の将軍坂は、本当に江戸時代にタイムスリップしたかのような凄みがあります。ちなみに、画面左側の石垣から伸びている枝は桜の木です。あと少し季節が進めば、この無人の将軍坂に夜桜が彩りを添える、さらに息を呑むような絶景が見られることでしょう。

location.name: "Shogun Slope" (Stone steps leading to Ha-no-Mon Gate)

27.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN

28.title: The Shachihoko and City Lights from the Keep
28.subtitle: A Gaze Across Time / Looking South from the Top Floor of the Main Keep
28.summary: Looking south from the top floor of the Main Keep. The glittering night view of Himeji city spreads beyond the illuminated Shachihoko. It is a spectacular view that evokes historical romance by contrasting today's bright city lights with the pitch-black castle town the feudal lords once saw.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: The top floor of the Main Keep, Himeji Castle (South-facing window)

tips.label: Castle Secret
tips.text: Do you know how many Shachihoko are on the Main Keep? There aren't just the two at the very top; there are actually 11 in total! Because of the complex, asymmetrical roof structure connecting the keeps, it ends up being an unusual odd number.

28.story:
I took this shot from a south-facing window after climbing to the top floor of the Main Keep. An illuminated Shachihoko sits in the foreground, with the glittering night view of Himeji city spreading out behind it.

Ideally, I would have loved to capture the massive pair of Shachihoko on the very top roof ridge, but since I obviously couldn't climb onto the roof (laughs), I made this one right outside the window my main subject!

Although the tile itself has been restored, successive lords of Himeji Castle must have looked down at the town below from this exact same direction. Thinking about the stark contrast between the pitch-black darkness the powerful rulers of centuries past would have seen, and the dazzling modern nightscape spreading before my eyes today, fills me with deep emotion, making me realize the vast passage of time.

By the way, as mentioned in the tips, there are 11 Shachihoko on the Main Keep. Interestingly, during the major Showa-era restoration, they used the oldest surviving Edo-period tile (which happened to be a closed-mouth female) as a mold for all of them. Thus, for a time, all 11 tiles had the exact same female face! (The top pair was finally corrected to a proper male-female pair during the recent Heisei restoration.)
28.story_jp:
大天守の最上階まで登り、南向きの窓から撮影した一枚です。手前にライトアップされた鯱瓦、その後ろには姫路の街の煌びやかな夜景が広がっています。

本当ならば一番上の大棟に乗っている巨大な二対の鯱瓦を撮りたいところなのですが、さすがに屋根に登るわけにはいきませんので（笑）、すぐ窓の向こうに見えるこの鯱を主役に据えました。

瓦自体は修理の際に新しく作り直されたものですが、歴代の城主たちも、これと全く同じ方角から眼下の城下町を見下ろしていたはずです。数百年前の権力者たちが見ていたであろう漆黒の闇と、いま目の前に広がる現代の眩い夜景。そのコントラストに思いを馳せると、時の流れの長さを実感し、非常に感慨深いものがあります。

ちなみに、先ほどのTipsで大天守には「11尾」の鯱がいると書きましたが、昭和の大修理でこれらを新調する際、一番古い江戸時代の鯱（口を閉じたメスの形）をモデルにすべての型をとったため、一時期「11尾すべてが同じメスの顔」になっていたという面白い歴史があります（※現在の一番上の鯱は、平成の修理で正しいオスメスのペアに直されています）。

location.name: Top floor of the Main Keep, Himeji Castle

28.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN

29.title: Hishi no Mon: Flower of Momoyama and Memories of Opening the Gate
29.subtitle: Touching History at Night / In front of Hishi no Mon
29.summary: The largest gate in the castle, "Hishi no Mon" (Diamond Gate), welcomes you just past the ticket booth. This is an unforgettable record of a night when I physically opened and closed this masterpiece of Azuchi-Momoyama architecture during a special tour.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Hishi no Mon (Diamond Gate), Himeji Castle (Right after the entrance)

tips.label: Photo Tips
tips.text: Focus on the elegant curves of the bell-shaped Katomado window on the second floor and the striking contrast between the black lacquered lattice windows and the brilliant white plaster to highlight the gate's elegance.

tips.label: Exclusive Experience
tips.text: During the annual "Premium Night Tour," participants are granted the incredibly rare opportunity to physically push open and close this historic gate with their own hands.

29.story:
Just past the ticket gates, as you head toward the heart of Himeji Castle, the first thing to welcome you is this magnificent structure: the "Hishi no Mon" (Diamond Gate).

While many of the castle's gates are purely practical and austere, this one vividly retains the glamorous architectural style of the Azuchi-Momoyama period. The elegant curves of the bell-shaped Katomado window on the second floor, paired with the striking contrast between the black lacquered lattice windows and the bright white plaster, are truly breathtaking.

Actually, during the "Himeji Castle Premium Night Tour" when this photo was taken, our group split into two and we were allowed to physically experience opening and closing this massive gate ourselves. The physical weight and profound emotion of moving an authentic castle gate with hundreds of years of history by my own hands are deeply etched in my memory. It is a highly memorable experience—and photograph—that allowed me not just to admire a beautiful building, but to directly touch history.
29.story_jp:
料金所を抜けて、姫路城の中枢へと向かう道すがら、最初に私たちを迎えてくれるのがこの立派な「菱の門」です。

実戦的で無骨な門が多い姫路城の中で、この門は安土桃山時代の華やかな建築様式を色濃く残しています。特に、2階部分にある釣り鐘のような形をした「火灯窓（かとうまど）」の曲線美や、黒漆塗りの格子窓と全体を覆う白漆喰のコントラストは、ため息が出るほど見事です。

実は、この写真を撮影した「姫路城プレミアムナイトツアー」では、参加者が二手に分かれて、実際にこの重厚な門の「開門」と「閉門」を体験させていただきました。数百年の歴史を持つ本物の城門を自らの手で動かしたあの瞬間のずっしりとした重みと感動は、今でも私の心に強く焼き付いています。ただ美しい建物を眺めるだけでなく、歴史に直接触れることができた、非常に思い出深い体験と一枚です。

location.name: Hishi no Mon (Diamond Gate), Himeji Castle

29.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN

30.title: Waves of Roof Tiles — The Keep Rises
30.subtitle: The Main Keep Through Layers of Castle Rooftops
30.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN
30.summary: Layers of turret and watchtower roofs overlap in succession, with the Main Keep rising grandly behind them. The undulating tile rooftops convey the magnificent scale of castle architecture.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: West side of Himeji Castle grounds

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: Using a telephoto focal length compresses the overlapping roofs for an impactful composition.

tips.label: Point of Interest
tips.text: Himeji Castle's connected tower complex consists of the Main Keep, three small keeps, and connecting corridors — a beautifully intricate architectural ensemble.

30.story:
From foreground to background, waves of tiled roofs overlap one upon another, culminating at the grand Main Keep that rises at their peak. This composition captures the very essence of Himeji Castle's "connected tower complex" — its defining architectural achievement.

The rhythmic succession of white walls and dark tiles repeats like a visual melody. Each roofline differs subtly in angle and curvature, harmonizing like musical voices as they guide the eye toward the Main Keep.

In the gentle late-afternoon light, each tile casts a soft shadow, crisply defining the contours of every structure. The texture of tiles that have endured centuries of wind and rain quietly speaks to the profound weight of this castle's history.
30.story_jp:
手前から奥へ、瓦屋根の波が幾重にも重なり、その頂点に大天守がそびえ立つ。この構図は、姫路城の「連立式天守群」という城郭建築としての真髄を、ひと目で伝えてくれます。

白壁と黒い瓦のコントラストが繰り返されるリズミカルな屋根の連なり。それぞれの屋根の角度や曲線が微妙に異なり、まるで音楽のハーモニーのように調和しながら大天守へと視線を導きます。

夕方に近い穏やかな光の中で、瓦の一枚一枚が柔らかな影を落とし、建物の輪郭をくっきりと浮かび上がらせています。何百年もの風雨に耐えてきた瓦たちの質感が、この城の歴史の重みを静かに語りかけてきます。

31.title: Castle Town Twilight — View from the Keep
31.subtitle: Himeji City and Sunset Sky from the Main Keep
31.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN
31.summary: Looking down from the upper floors of the Main Keep, Otemae-dori avenue stretches straight toward Himeji Station under a sky painted in pink and purple — a special panorama that evokes the view once seen by castle lords.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Upper floors of Himeji Castle Main Keep

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: The magic hour around sunset produces the most beautiful light. Shoot through the lattice windows by positioning your lens close to the bars and focusing on the background.

tips.label: Visiting Info
tips.text: The top floor of the keep is accessible during regular visiting hours. Late afternoon tends to be less crowded.

31.story:
Standing on an upper floor of the Main Keep and looking south, the grand Otemae-dori avenue stretches in a straight line toward Himeji Station, revealing the full expanse of the castle town below. Buildings and streetlights along the avenue begin to glow in the fading twilight, while the silhouettes of trees in San-no-Maru Square create a gentle foreground.

Pink and pale purple clouds spread across the sky, softly embracing the city of Himeji as it transitions from day to night. When the castle lords once looked down from this very spot, they would have seen an expanse of traditional tiled rooftops stretching into the distance. Though eras have passed and buildings have changed, the essence of watching over the town from this elevated vantage point remains unchanged.

A castle town viewed from atop the stone walls of its keep — it is a deeply moving sight where history and the present quietly overlap, transcending words.
31.story_jp:
大天守の上階に立ち、南を見下ろすと、大手前通りが姫路駅へと一直線に伸びる城下町の姿が広がります。通りの両側に立ち並ぶビルや街灯が、夕暮れ時の薄明かりの中で輝き始め、三の丸広場の木々のシルエットが穏やかな前景を形作っています。

空にはピンクと薄紫の雲が広がり、昼から夜へと移り変わる姫路の街を優しく包み込んでいます。かつてこの同じ場所から城主が見下ろした時、眼下に広がっていたのは瓦屋根の連なる城下町だったことでしょう。時代は移り、建物は変わっても、この高台から街を見守る眺めの本質は変わりません。

石垣の上に立つ天守から見下ろす城下町。それは、歴史と現在が静かに重なり合う、言葉にし難い感動の眺めです。

32.title: Blue Sky Ascent — White Peaks of the Keep
32.subtitle: The Dramatic Power of the Tower Complex from Below
32.gear: Leica SL2-S + Sigma 24-70mm F2.8 DG DN
32.summary: Looking up from the base of the stone walls under a blue sky, the keep's tower complex — white walls and black tiles layered upon each other — rises with the overwhelming grandeur of a white mountain range.

tips.label: Location
tips.text: Near Bizen-maru, Himeji Castle

tips.label: Photo Tip
tips.text: A wide-angle lens capturing from the stone walls to the keep's summit conveys the full impact of its height.

tips.label: Point of Interest
tips.text: The view from Bizen-maru looking up is one of the best spots to truly feel the enormous scale of the keep.

32.story:
Looking straight up from the base of the stone walls, the tower complex — layers upon layers of white walls and black-tiled roofs thrusting skyward — fills your field of vision with overwhelming scale.

From this low angle, the sheer height and multi-layered complexity of Himeji Castle are conveyed without reservation. Up close, the Main Keep's walls are staggeringly massive, the white plaster dazzling against the blue sky. Each level shifts at a subtly different angle as they stack upward — a structure truly worthy of being called "architectural art."

This is a completely different experience from admiring the castle's graceful silhouette from afar. The overwhelming power felt from its very foundations makes you deeply understand why this castle has inspired awe in people for hundreds of years.
32.story_jp:
石垣の足元から真上を見上げると、白壁と黒い瓦屋根が幾層にも重なり合いながら青空へと突き上げる天守群の姿が、圧倒的なスケールで目に飛び込んできます。

下から見上げるこのアングルでは、姫路城の「高さ」と「重層性」が余すところなく伝わってきます。大天守の壁面は、近くで見ると驚くほど巨大で、白漆喰の壁が青空に映えて眩しいほどです。それぞれの階が微妙に角度を変えながら上へ上へと積み重なっていく構造は、まさに「建築の芸術」と呼ぶにふさわしい美しさです。

遠くから眺める優美な姿とは異なる、足元から感じる圧倒的な迫力。この城が何百年もの間、人々を畏敬させてきた理由が、この見上げた瞬間に深く実感できます。
